revision.comment: Text replacement - "[[Category:automatic wikipedia redirects]]" to "<nowiki>[[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]</nowiki>"

revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} {{High-use}} {{Lua|Module:In lang}} '''Template:In lang''' is used to denote that a text source is written in a specific language. For citations using a citation template (such as {{tlx|cite web}}, {{tlx|cite news}}, {{tlx|cite journal}}, etc.), that template's {{Para|language}} parameter should be used instead. To note a span of text in a different language, {{tlx|lang}} or {{tlx|langx}}, should be used instead. ==Usage== Typical use of this template is inside {{tag|ref}} tags where the reference is not templated and the referenced source is non-English: :[https://www.example.com "Non English Journal Article"]. ''Non-English Journal''. '''12'''(3): 231–241 {{in lang|xx}}. Also finds use in External links sections to mark non-English link-targets: :[https://www.example.com Non English external link] {{in lang|xx}} This template does not markup non-English text. For that, use {{tlx|lang}} or {{tlx|langx}} templates. ===Parameters=== This template accepts one or more positional language-tag parameters (<{{var|tag}}>) and two named parameters: *{{tld|In lang|<{{var|tag}}>|<{{var|tag2}}>|{{var|...}}|link{{=}}|cap{{=}}}} Most common use is a single language: *{{tld|In lang|de}} → {{In lang|de}} The positional parameters * <{{var|tag}}> – required; <{{var|tag}}> is a valid [[ISO-639]] language tag or a valid [[IETF language tag]]; more than one language tag supported: ** {{In lang|cs|en|de|fr|es|ca-valencia|pl|ru|ja|zh}} → *** {{In lang|cs|en|de|fr|es|ca-valencia|pl|ru|ja|zh}} The named parameters are: * link – accepts the single value yes; creates link to language article ** {{tlc|In lang|nv|link{{=}}yes}} → {{code |lang=wikitext|{{In lang|nv|link=yes}}}} → {{In lang|nv|link=yes}} * cap – accepts the single value yes; capitalizes the first letter of "In": ** {{tlc|In lang|pt-BR|cap{{=}}yes}} → {{In lang|pt-BR|cap=yes}} ===Error messages=== This template has one error message of its own: :error: {{In lang}} missing language tag – displayed when the template is transcluded without an ISO 639 language tag or IETF language tag. All other error messages related to the use of this template are emitted by [[Module:Lang]] and are documented at {{cl|Lang and lang-xx template errors}}. ==TemplateData== {{TemplateData header}} { "params": { "1": {}, "2": {}, "3": {}, "4": {} }, "description": "Produces the phrase \"(in LANGUAGE)\" with language codes.", "format": "inline" } ==Tracking categories== Transclusions in mainspace articles will add the article to the appropriate subcategory of {{cl|Articles with non-English-language sources}}. There are two forms of these subcategories: :Category:Articles with <{{var|language name}}>-language sources (<{{var|tag}}>) – for individual languages{{cite web |url=https://iso639-3.sil.org/about/scope#Individual%20languages |title=ISO 639-3: Scope of denotation for language identifiers: Individual languages |website=SIL International |access-date=5 December 2019}} and for macrolanguages{{cite web |url=https://iso639-3.sil.org/about/scope#Macrolanguages |title=ISO 639-3: Scope of denotation for language identifiers: Macrolanguages |website=SIL International |access-date=5 December 2019}} :Category:Articles with sources in <{{var|collective name}}> languages (<{{var|tag}}>) – for language collectives{{cite web |url=https://iso639-3.sil.org/about/scope#Collections%20of%20languages |title=ISO 639-3: Scope of denotation for language identifiers: Collections of languages |website=SIL International |access-date=5 December 2019}} where <{{var|language name}}> and <{{var|collective name}}> is the name used in the template's rendering and <{{var|tag}}> is the ISO 639 tag or IETF language tag. ==References== {{Reflist}} {{Sandbox other|| [[Category:Language templates]] [[Category:Wikipedia multilingual support templates]] [[Category:Language tag handling templates]] }}

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} {{High-use}} {{Lua|Module:In lang}} '''Template:In lang''' is used to denote that a text source is written in a specific language. For citations using a citation template (such as {{tlx|cite web}}, {{tlx|cite news}}, {{tlx|cite journal}}, etc.), that template's {{Para|language}} parameter should be used instead. To note a span of text in a different language, {{tlx|lang}} or {{tlx|langx}}, should be used instead. ==Usage== Typical use of this template is inside {{tag|ref}} tags where the reference is not templated and the referenced source is non-English: :[https://www.example.com "Non English Journal Article"]. ''Non-English Journal''. '''12'''(3): 231–241 {{in lang|xx}}. Also finds use in External links sections to mark non-English link-targets: :[https://www.example.com Non English external link] {{in lang|xx}} This template does not markup non-English text. For that, use {{tlx|lang}} or {{tlx|langx}} templates. ===Parameters=== This template accepts one or more positional language-tag parameters (<{{var|tag}}>) and two named parameters: *{{tld|In lang|<{{var|tag}}>|<{{var|tag2}}>|{{var|...}}|link{{=}}|cap{{=}}}} Most common use is a single language: *{{tld|In lang|de}} → {{In lang|de}} The positional parameters * <{{var|tag}}> – required; <{{var|tag}}> is a valid [[ISO-639]] language tag or a valid [[:en:IETF language tag|IETF language tag]]; more than one language tag supported: ** {{In lang|cs|en|de|fr|es|ca-valencia|pl|ru|ja|zh}} → *** {{In lang|cs|en|de|fr|es|ca-valencia|pl|ru|ja|zh}} The named parameters are: * link – accepts the single value yes; creates link to language article ** {{tlc|In lang|nv|link{{=}}yes}} → {{code |lang=wikitext|{{In lang|nv|link=yes}}}} → {{In lang|nv|link=yes}} * cap – accepts the single value yes; capitalizes the first letter of "In": ** {{tlc|In lang|pt-BR|cap{{=}}yes}} → {{In lang|pt-BR|cap=yes}} ===Error messages=== This template has one error message of its own: :error: {{In lang}} missing language tag – displayed when the template is transcluded without an ISO 639 language tag or IETF language tag. All other error messages related to the use of this template are emitted by [[Module:Lang]] and are documented at {{cl|Lang and lang-xx template errors}}. ==TemplateData== {{TemplateData header}} { "params": { "1": {}, "2": {}, "3": {}, "4": {} }, "description": "Produces the phrase \"(in LANGUAGE)\" with language codes.", "format": "inline" } ==Tracking categories== Transclusions in mainspace articles will add the article to the appropriate subcategory of {{cl|Articles with non-English-language sources}}. There are two forms of these subcategories: :Category:Articles with <{{var|language name}}>-language sources (<{{var|tag}}>) – for individual languages{{cite web |url=https://iso639-3.sil.org/about/scope#Individual%20languages |title=ISO 639-3: Scope of denotation for language identifiers: Individual languages |website=SIL International |access-date=5 December 2019}} and for macrolanguages{{cite web |url=https://iso639-3.sil.org/about/scope#Macrolanguages |title=ISO 639-3: Scope of denotation for language identifiers: Macrolanguages |website=SIL International |access-date=5 December 2019}} :Category:Articles with sources in <{{var|collective name}}> languages (<{{var|tag}}>) – for language collectives{{cite web |url=https://iso639-3.sil.org/about/scope#Collections%20of%20languages |title=ISO 639-3: Scope of denotation for language identifiers: Collections of languages |website=SIL International |access-date=5 December 2019}} where <{{var|language name}}> and <{{var|collective name}}> is the name used in the template's rendering and <{{var|tag}}> is the ISO 639 tag or IETF language tag. ==References== {{Reflist}} {{Sandbox other|| [[Category:Language templates]] [[Category:Wikipedia multilingual support templates]] [[Category:Language tag handling templates]] }}

revision.comment: Bot: convert local/ILL link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} {{High-use}} {{Lua|Module:In lang}} '''Template:In lang''' is used to denote that a text source is written in a specific language. For citations using a citation template (such as {{tlx|cite web}}, {{tlx|cite news}}, {{tlx|cite journal}}, etc.), that template's {{Para|language}} parameter should be used instead. To note a span of text in a different language, {{tlx|lang}} or {{tlx|langx}}, should be used instead. ==Usage== Typical use of this template is inside {{tag|ref}} tags where the reference is not templated and the referenced source is non-English: :[https://www.example.com "Non English Journal Article"]. ''Non-English Journal''. '''12'''(3): 231–241 {{in lang|xx}}. Also finds use in External links sections to mark non-English link-targets: :[https://www.example.com Non English external link] {{in lang|xx}} This template does not markup non-English text. For that, use {{tlx|lang}} or {{tlx|langx}} templates. ===Parameters=== This template accepts one or more positional language-tag parameters (<{{var|tag}}>) and two named parameters: *{{tld|In lang|<{{var|tag}}>|<{{var|tag2}}>|{{var|...}}|link{{=}}|cap{{=}}}} Most common use is a single language: *{{tld|In lang|de}} → {{In lang|de}} The positional parameters * <{{var|tag}}> – required; <{{var|tag}}> is a valid [[:en:ISO-639|ISO-639]] language tag or a valid [[:en:IETF language tag|IETF language tag]]; more than one language tag supported: ** {{In lang|cs|en|de|fr|es|ca-valencia|pl|ru|ja|zh}} → *** {{In lang|cs|en|de|fr|es|ca-valencia|pl|ru|ja|zh}} The named parameters are: * link – accepts the single value yes; creates link to language article ** {{tlc|In lang|nv|link{{=}}yes}} → {{code |lang=wikitext|{{In lang|nv|link=yes}}}} → {{In lang|nv|link=yes}} * cap – accepts the single value yes; capitalizes the first letter of "In": ** {{tlc|In lang|pt-BR|cap{{=}}yes}} → {{In lang|pt-BR|cap=yes}} ===Error messages=== This template has one error message of its own: :error: {{In lang}} missing language tag – displayed when the template is transcluded without an ISO 639 language tag or IETF language tag. All other error messages related to the use of this template are emitted by [[Module:Lang]] and are documented at {{cl|Lang and lang-xx template errors}}. ==TemplateData== {{TemplateData header}} { "params": { "1": {}, "2": {}, "3": {}, "4": {} }, "description": "Produces the phrase \"(in LANGUAGE)\" with language codes.", "format": "inline" } ==Tracking categories== Transclusions in mainspace articles will add the article to the appropriate subcategory of {{cl|Articles with non-English-language sources}}. There are two forms of these subcategories: :Category:Articles with <{{var|language name}}>-language sources (<{{var|tag}}>) – for individual languages{{cite web |url=https://iso639-3.sil.org/about/scope#Individual%20languages |title=ISO 639-3: Scope of denotation for language identifiers: Individual languages |website=SIL International |access-date=5 December 2019}} and for macrolanguages{{cite web |url=https://iso639-3.sil.org/about/scope#Macrolanguages |title=ISO 639-3: Scope of denotation for language identifiers: Macrolanguages |website=SIL International |access-date=5 December 2019}} :Category:Articles with sources in <{{var|collective name}}> languages (<{{var|tag}}>) – for language collectives{{cite web |url=https://iso639-3.sil.org/about/scope#Collections%20of%20languages |title=ISO 639-3: Scope of denotation for language identifiers: Collections of languages |website=SIL International |access-date=5 December 2019}} where <{{var|language name}}> and <{{var|collective name}}> is the name used in the template's rendering and <{{var|tag}}> is the ISO 639 tag or IETF language tag. ==References== {{Reflist}} {{Sandbox other|| [[Category:Language templates]] [[Category:Wikipedia multilingual support templates]] [[Category:Language tag handling templates]] }}

revision.comment: Bot: add {{wikidata link|Q...}} or [[Category:Templates without wikidata]]
revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} {{High-use}} {{Lua|Module:In lang}} '''Template:In lang''' is used to denote that a text source is written in a specific language. For citations using a citation template (such as {{tlx|cite web}}, {{tlx|cite news}}, {{tlx|cite journal}}, etc.), that template's {{Para|language}} parameter should be used instead. To note a span of text in a different language, {{tlx|lang}} or {{tlx|langx}}, should be used instead. ==Usage== Typical use of this template is inside {{tag|ref}} tags where the reference is not templated and the referenced source is non-English: :[https://www.example.com "Non English Journal Article"]. ''Non-English Journal''. '''12'''(3): 231–241 {{in lang|xx}}. Also finds use in External links sections to mark non-English link-targets: :[https://www.example.com Non English external link] {{in lang|xx}} This template does not markup non-English text. For that, use {{tlx|lang}} or {{tlx|langx}} templates. ===Parameters=== This template accepts one or more positional language-tag parameters (<{{var|tag}}>) and two named parameters: *{{tld|In lang|<{{var|tag}}>|<{{var|tag2}}>|{{var|...}}|link{{=}}|cap{{=}}}} Most common use is a single language: *{{tld|In lang|de}} → {{In lang|de}} The positional parameters * <{{var|tag}}> – required; <{{var|tag}}> is a valid [[:en:ISO-639|ISO-639]] language tag or a valid [[:en:IETF language tag|IETF language tag]]; more than one language tag supported: ** {{In lang|cs|en|de|fr|es|ca-valencia|pl|ru|ja|zh}} → *** {{In lang|cs|en|de|fr|es|ca-valencia|pl|ru|ja|zh}} The named parameters are: * link – accepts the single value yes; creates link to language article ** {{tlc|In lang|nv|link{{=}}yes}} → {{code |lang=wikitext|{{In lang|nv|link=yes}}}} → {{In lang|nv|link=yes}} * cap – accepts the single value yes; capitalizes the first letter of "In": ** {{tlc|In lang|pt-BR|cap{{=}}yes}} → {{In lang|pt-BR|cap=yes}} ===Error messages=== This template has one error message of its own: :error: {{In lang}} missing language tag – displayed when the template is transcluded without an ISO 639 language tag or IETF language tag. All other error messages related to the use of this template are emitted by [[Module:Lang]] and are documented at {{cl|Lang and lang-xx template errors}}. ==TemplateData== {{TemplateData header}} { "params": { "1": {}, "2": {}, "3": {}, "4": {} }, "description": "Produces the phrase \"(in LANGUAGE)\" with language codes.", "format": "inline" } ==Tracking categories== Transclusions in mainspace articles will add the article to the appropriate subcategory of {{cl|Articles with non-English-language sources}}. There are two forms of these subcategories: :Category:Articles with <{{var|language name}}>-language sources (<{{var|tag}}>) – for individual languages{{cite web |url=https://iso639-3.sil.org/about/scope#Individual%20languages |title=ISO 639-3: Scope of denotation for language identifiers: Individual languages |website=SIL International |access-date=5 December 2019}} and for macrolanguages{{cite web |url=https://iso639-3.sil.org/about/scope#Macrolanguages |title=ISO 639-3: Scope of denotation for language identifiers: Macrolanguages |website=SIL International |access-date=5 December 2019}} :Category:Articles with sources in <{{var|collective name}}> languages (<{{var|tag}}>) – for language collectives{{cite web |url=https://iso639-3.sil.org/about/scope#Collections%20of%20languages |title=ISO 639-3: Scope of denotation for language identifiers: Collections of languages |website=SIL International |access-date=5 December 2019}} where <{{var|language name}}> and <{{var|collective name}}> is the name used in the template's rendering and <{{var|tag}}> is the ISO 639 tag or IETF language tag. ==References== {{Reflist}} {{Sandbox other|| [[Category:Language templates]] [[Category:Wikipedia multilingual support templates]] [[Category:Language tag handling templates]] }} [[Category:Templates without wikidata]]

revision.comment: imported only latest revision from https://archive.org/details/miraheze-wiki-shintowiki-20251218 to avoid excessive disk usage, comment on the revision was Text replacement - "↵<noinclude>[[Category:Templates without wikidata]]</noinclude>" to ""
revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} {{High-use}} {{Lua|Module:In lang}} '''Template:In lang''' is used to denote that a text source is written in a specific language. For citations using a citation template (such as {{tlx|cite web}}, {{tlx|cite news}}, {{tlx|cite journal}}, etc.), that template's {{Para|language}} parameter should be used instead. To note a span of text in a different language, {{tlx|lang}} or {{tlx|langx}}, should be used instead. ==Usage== Typical use of this template is inside {{tag|ref}} tags where the reference is not templated and the referenced source is non-English: :[https://www.example.com "Non English Journal Article"]. ''Non-English Journal''. '''12'''(3): 231–241 {{in lang|xx}}. Also finds use in External links sections to mark non-English link-targets: :[https://www.example.com Non English external link] {{in lang|xx}} This template does not markup non-English text. For that, use {{tlx|lang}} or {{tlx|langx}} templates. ===Parameters=== This template accepts one or more positional language-tag parameters (<{{var|tag}}>) and two named parameters: *{{tld|In lang|<{{var|tag}}>|<{{var|tag2}}>|{{var|...}}|link{{=}}|cap{{=}}}} Most common use is a single language: *{{tld|In lang|de}} → {{In lang|de}} The positional parameters * <{{var|tag}}> – required; <{{var|tag}}> is a valid [[:en:ISO-639|ISO-639]] language tag or a valid [[:en:IETF language tag|IETF language tag]]; more than one language tag supported: ** {{In lang|cs|en|de|fr|es|ca-valencia|pl|ru|ja|zh}} → *** {{In lang|cs|en|de|fr|es|ca-valencia|pl|ru|ja|zh}} The named parameters are: * link – accepts the single value yes; creates link to language article ** {{tlc|In lang|nv|link{{=}}yes}} → {{code |lang=wikitext|{{In lang|nv|link=yes}}}} → {{In lang|nv|link=yes}} * cap – accepts the single value yes; capitalizes the first letter of "In": ** {{tlc|In lang|pt-BR|cap{{=}}yes}} → {{In lang|pt-BR|cap=yes}} ===Error messages=== This template has one error message of its own: :error: {{In lang}} missing language tag – displayed when the template is transcluded without an ISO 639 language tag or IETF language tag. All other error messages related to the use of this template are emitted by [[Module:Lang]] and are documented at {{cl|Lang and lang-xx template errors}}. ==TemplateData== {{TemplateData header}} { "params": { "1": {}, "2": {}, "3": {}, "4": {} }, "description": "Produces the phrase \"(in LANGUAGE)\" with language codes.", "format": "inline" } ==Tracking categories== Transclusions in mainspace articles will add the article to the appropriate subcategory of {{cl|Articles with non-English-language sources}}. There are two forms of these subcategories: :Category:Articles with <{{var|language name}}>-language sources (<{{var|tag}}>) – for individual languages{{cite web |url=https://iso639-3.sil.org/about/scope#Individual%20languages |title=ISO 639-3: Scope of denotation for language identifiers: Individual languages |website=SIL International |access-date=5 December 2019}} and for macrolanguages{{cite web |url=https://iso639-3.sil.org/about/scope#Macrolanguages |title=ISO 639-3: Scope of denotation for language identifiers: Macrolanguages |website=SIL International |access-date=5 December 2019}} :Category:Articles with sources in <{{var|collective name}}> languages (<{{var|tag}}>) – for language collectives{{cite web |url=https://iso639-3.sil.org/about/scope#Collections%20of%20languages |title=ISO 639-3: Scope of denotation for language identifiers: Collections of languages |website=SIL International |access-date=5 December 2019}} where <{{var|language name}}> and <{{var|collective name}}> is the name used in the template's rendering and <{{var|tag}}> is the ISO 639 tag or IETF language tag. ==References== {{Reflist}} {{Sandbox other|| [[Category:Language templates]] [[Category:Wikipedia multilingual support templates]] [[Category:Language tag handling templates]] }}

revision.comment: Bot: Adding dummy category to make categories work after wiki restoration
revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} {{High-use}} {{Lua|Module:In lang}} '''Template:In lang''' is used to denote that a text source is written in a specific language. For citations using a citation template (such as {{tlx|cite web}}, {{tlx|cite news}}, {{tlx|cite journal}}, etc.), that template's {{Para|language}} parameter should be used instead. To note a span of text in a different language, {{tlx|lang}} or {{tlx|langx}}, should be used instead. ==Usage== Typical use of this template is inside {{tag|ref}} tags where the reference is not templated and the referenced source is non-English: :[https://www.example.com "Non English Journal Article"]. ''Non-English Journal''. '''12'''(3): 231–241 {{in lang|xx}}. Also finds use in External links sections to mark non-English link-targets: :[https://www.example.com Non English external link] {{in lang|xx}} This template does not markup non-English text. For that, use {{tlx|lang}} or {{tlx|langx}} templates. ===Parameters=== This template accepts one or more positional language-tag parameters (<{{var|tag}}>) and two named parameters: *{{tld|In lang|<{{var|tag}}>|<{{var|tag2}}>|{{var|...}}|link{{=}}|cap{{=}}}} Most common use is a single language: *{{tld|In lang|de}} → {{In lang|de}} The positional parameters * <{{var|tag}}> – required; <{{var|tag}}> is a valid [[:en:ISO-639|ISO-639]] language tag or a valid [[:en:IETF language tag|IETF language tag]]; more than one language tag supported: ** {{In lang|cs|en|de|fr|es|ca-valencia|pl|ru|ja|zh}} → *** {{In lang|cs|en|de|fr|es|ca-valencia|pl|ru|ja|zh}} The named parameters are: * link – accepts the single value yes; creates link to language article ** {{tlc|In lang|nv|link{{=}}yes}} → {{code |lang=wikitext|{{In lang|nv|link=yes}}}} → {{In lang|nv|link=yes}} * cap – accepts the single value yes; capitalizes the first letter of "In": ** {{tlc|In lang|pt-BR|cap{{=}}yes}} → {{In lang|pt-BR|cap=yes}} ===Error messages=== This template has one error message of its own: :error: {{In lang}} missing language tag – displayed when the template is transcluded without an ISO 639 language tag or IETF language tag. All other error messages related to the use of this template are emitted by [[Module:Lang]] and are documented at {{cl|Lang and lang-xx template errors}}. ==TemplateData== {{TemplateData header}} { "params": { "1": {}, "2": {}, "3": {}, "4": {} }, "description": "Produces the phrase \"(in LANGUAGE)\" with language codes.", "format": "inline" } ==Tracking categories== Transclusions in mainspace articles will add the article to the appropriate subcategory of {{cl|Articles with non-English-language sources}}. There are two forms of these subcategories: :Category:Articles with <{{var|language name}}>-language sources (<{{var|tag}}>) – for individual languages{{cite web |url=https://iso639-3.sil.org/about/scope#Individual%20languages |title=ISO 639-3: Scope of denotation for language identifiers: Individual languages |website=SIL International |access-date=5 December 2019}} and for macrolanguages{{cite web |url=https://iso639-3.sil.org/about/scope#Macrolanguages |title=ISO 639-3: Scope of denotation for language identifiers: Macrolanguages |website=SIL International |access-date=5 December 2019}} :Category:Articles with sources in <{{var|collective name}}> languages (<{{var|tag}}>) – for language collectives{{cite web |url=https://iso639-3.sil.org/about/scope#Collections%20of%20languages |title=ISO 639-3: Scope of denotation for language identifiers: Collections of languages |website=SIL International |access-date=5 December 2019}} where <{{var|language name}}> and <{{var|collective name}}> is the name used in the template's rendering and <{{var|tag}}> is the ISO 639 tag or IETF language tag. ==References== {{Reflist}} {{Sandbox other|| [[Category:Language templates]] [[Category:Wikipedia multilingual support templates]] [[Category:Language tag handling templates]] }} [[Category:Templates]]

revision.comment: Bot: wrap 4 stray tag(s) in noinclude [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23458359115|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} {{High-use}} {{Lua|Module:In lang}} '''Template:In lang''' is used to denote that a text source is written in a specific language. For citations using a citation template (such as {{tlx|cite web}}, {{tlx|cite news}}, {{tlx|cite journal}}, etc.), that template's {{Para|language}} parameter should be used instead. To note a span of text in a different language, {{tlx|lang}} or {{tlx|langx}}, should be used instead. ==Usage== Typical use of this template is inside {{tag|ref}} tags where the reference is not templated and the referenced source is non-English: :[https://www.example.com "Non English Journal Article"]. ''Non-English Journal''. '''12'''(3): 231–241 {{in lang|xx}}. Also finds use in External links sections to mark non-English link-targets: :[https://www.example.com Non English external link] {{in lang|xx}} This template does not markup non-English text. For that, use {{tlx|lang}} or {{tlx|langx}} templates. ===Parameters=== This template accepts one or more positional language-tag parameters (<{{var|tag}}>) and two named parameters: *{{tld|In lang|<{{var|tag}}>|<{{var|tag2}}>|{{var|...}}|link{{=}}|cap{{=}}}} Most common use is a single language: *{{tld|In lang|de}} → {{In lang|de}} The positional parameters * <{{var|tag}}> – required; <{{var|tag}}> is a valid [[:en:ISO-639|ISO-639]] language tag or a valid [[:en:IETF language tag|IETF language tag]]; more than one language tag supported: ** {{In lang|cs|en|de|fr|es|ca-valencia|pl|ru|ja|zh}} → *** {{In lang|cs|en|de|fr|es|ca-valencia|pl|ru|ja|zh}} The named parameters are: * link – accepts the single value yes; creates link to language article ** {{tlc|In lang|nv|link{{=}}yes}} → {{code |lang=wikitext|{{In lang|nv|link=yes}}}} → {{In lang|nv|link=yes}} * cap – accepts the single value yes; capitalizes the first letter of "In": ** {{tlc|In lang|pt-BR|cap{{=}}yes}} → {{In lang|pt-BR|cap=yes}} ===Error messages=== This template has one error message of its own: :error: {{In lang}} missing language tag – displayed when the template is transcluded without an ISO 639 language tag or IETF language tag. All other error messages related to the use of this template are emitted by [[Module:Lang]] and are documented at {{cl|Lang and lang-xx template errors}}. ==TemplateData== {{TemplateData header}} { "params": { "1": {}, "2": {}, "3": {}, "4": {} }, "description": "Produces the phrase \"(in LANGUAGE)\" with language codes.", "format": "inline" } ==Tracking categories== Transclusions in mainspace articles will add the article to the appropriate subcategory of {{cl|Articles with non-English-language sources}}. There are two forms of these subcategories: :Category:Articles with <{{var|language name}}>-language sources (<{{var|tag}}>) – for individual languages{{cite web |url=https://iso639-3.sil.org/about/scope#Individual%20languages |title=ISO 639-3: Scope of denotation for language identifiers: Individual languages |website=SIL International |access-date=5 December 2019}} and for macrolanguages{{cite web |url=https://iso639-3.sil.org/about/scope#Macrolanguages |title=ISO 639-3: Scope of denotation for language identifiers: Macrolanguages |website=SIL International |access-date=5 December 2019}} :Category:Articles with sources in <{{var|collective name}}> languages (<{{var|tag}}>) – for language collectives{{cite web |url=https://iso639-3.sil.org/about/scope#Collections%20of%20languages |title=ISO 639-3: Scope of denotation for language identifiers: Collections of languages |website=SIL International |access-date=5 December 2019}} where <{{var|language name}}> and <{{var|collective name}}> is the name used in the template's rendering and <{{var|tag}}> is the ISO 639 tag or IETF language tag. ==References== {{Reflist}} {{Sandbox other|| }} [[Category:Language templates]] [[Category:Wikipedia multilingual support templates]] [[Category:Language tag handling templates]] [[Category:Templates]] [[Category:Templates fixed with noinclude]]

revision.comment: Bot: strip HTML comments [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24938264069|time triggered pipeline]]
revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} {{High-use}} {{Lua|Module:In lang}} '''Template:In lang''' is used to denote that a text source is written in a specific language. For citations using a citation template (such as {{tlx|cite web}}, {{tlx|cite news}}, {{tlx|cite journal}}, etc.), that template's {{Para|language}} parameter should be used instead. To note a span of text in a different language, {{tlx|lang}} or {{tlx|langx}}, should be used instead. ==Usage== Typical use of this template is inside {{tag|ref}} tags where the reference is not templated and the referenced source is non-English: :[https://www.example.com "Non English Journal Article"]. ''Non-English Journal''. '''12'''(3): 231–241 {{in lang|xx}}. Also finds use in External links sections to mark non-English link-targets: :[https://www.example.com Non English external link] {{in lang|xx}} This template does not markup non-English text. For that, use {{tlx|lang}} or {{tlx|langx}} templates. ===Parameters=== This template accepts one or more positional language-tag parameters (<{{var|tag}}>) and two named parameters: *{{tld|In lang|<{{var|tag}}>|<{{var|tag2}}>|{{var|...}}|link{{=}}|cap{{=}}}} Most common use is a single language: *{{tld|In lang|de}} → {{In lang|de}} The positional parameters * <{{var|tag}}> – required; <{{var|tag}}> is a valid [[:en:ISO-639|ISO-639]] language tag or a valid [[:en:IETF language tag|IETF language tag]]; more than one language tag supported: ** {{In lang|cs|en|de|fr|es|ca-valencia|pl|ru|ja|zh}} → *** {{In lang|cs|en|de|fr|es|ca-valencia|pl|ru|ja|zh}} The named parameters are: * link – accepts the single value yes; creates link to language article ** {{tlc|In lang|nv|link{{=}}yes}} → {{code |lang=wikitext|{{In lang|nv|link=yes}}}} → {{In lang|nv|link=yes}} * cap – accepts the single value yes; capitalizes the first letter of "In": ** {{tlc|In lang|pt-BR|cap{{=}}yes}} → {{In lang|pt-BR|cap=yes}} ===Error messages=== This template has one error message of its own: :error: {{In lang}} missing language tag – displayed when the template is transcluded without an ISO 639 language tag or IETF language tag. All other error messages related to the use of this template are emitted by [[Module:Lang]] and are documented at {{cl|Lang and lang-xx template errors}}. ==TemplateData== {{TemplateData header}} { "params": { "1": {}, "2": {}, "3": {}, "4": {} }, "description": "Produces the phrase \"(in LANGUAGE)\" with language codes.", "format": "inline" } ==Tracking categories== Transclusions in mainspace articles will add the article to the appropriate subcategory of {{cl|Articles with non-English-language sources}}. There are two forms of these subcategories: :Category:Articles with <{{var|language name}}>-language sources (<{{var|tag}}>) – for individual languages{{cite web |url=https://iso639-3.sil.org/about/scope#Individual%20languages |title=ISO 639-3: Scope of denotation for language identifiers: Individual languages |website=SIL International |access-date=5 December 2019}} and for macrolanguages{{cite web |url=https://iso639-3.sil.org/about/scope#Macrolanguages |title=ISO 639-3: Scope of denotation for language identifiers: Macrolanguages |website=SIL International |access-date=5 December 2019}} :Category:Articles with sources in <{{var|collective name}}> languages (<{{var|tag}}>) – for language collectives{{cite web |url=https://iso639-3.sil.org/about/scope#Collections%20of%20languages |title=ISO 639-3: Scope of denotation for language identifiers: Collections of languages |website=SIL International |access-date=5 December 2019}} where <{{var|language name}}> and <{{var|collective name}}> is the name used in the template's rendering and <{{var|tag}}> is the ISO 639 tag or IETF language tag. ==References== {{Reflist}} {{Sandbox other|| }} [[Category:Language templates]] [[Category:Wikipedia multilingual support templates]] [[Category:Language tag handling templates]] [[Category:Templates]] [[Category:Templates fixed with noinclude]]
